digest.component_or_area: attestation event store reducer + signing producer (op-certs)
propositions: Op-cert DSSE envelopes are stored on a ticket by extending the existing SIGNATURE event record with an optional envelope plus bound material_fingerprint/merged_log_commit, rather than adding a new event type.
A new producer sign_opcert_manifest appends an envelope-bearing SIGNATURE event that omits the HMAC signature/algorithm and carries a DSSE envelope built via opcert.sign_opcert.
The reducer process_signature must preserve envelope, material_fingerprint, and merged_log_commit into attestations[kind] present-only, surviving a full reduce and a compact-then-replay round-trip.
A read helper opcert.opcert_from_record returns (envelope, bound_fields) for op-cert records and None for legacy HMAC records, enabling verification via a pinned keyring.
The record-schema extension is additive: legacy HMAC sign_manifest records stay byte-unchanged and still HMAC-verify.
Op-cert events are preserved append-only but read as unsigned/unverifiable on pre-upgrade clones, so verify/reconcile hosts must be upgraded first.
